questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Secularism is a political system where the state does not favor any particular religion.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This is achieved by separating the church and state, making the state neutral and impartial towards all religions.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This is the correct explanation of secularism as it emphasizes the separation of church and state.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Secularism is a concept that originated in 16th century Europe.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It was first introduced in the Netherlands and then spread to other European countries.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Secularism indeed originated in 16th century Europe, and the Netherlands was one of the first countries to adopt it.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Secularism is not followed by all countries in the world.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Many countries have a state religion and the government interferes in religious matters.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Secularism is not followed by all countries, and many have a state religion or government interference in religious matters.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Secularism promotes the idea of religious neutrality in the public sphere.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This allows people of different religions to coexist peacefully and maintain their individual beliefs.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Secularism indeed promotes religious neutrality, allowing people of different religions to coexist peacefully.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Secularism is a concept that is limited to the political sphere.
